section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing
section13_checks.check_notes: The artifact's validation_status (CL-02) confirms the section schema source is the application form (af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf, form_version_part_b: 5.1), traced to Tier 2A docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/section_schema_registry.json. No strings matching 'Annex [0-9]', 'Grant Agreement Annex', 'AGA Template', or 'Model Grant Agreement template' used as a structural authority appear in either sub-section's content or traceability_footer. The 'General Assembly (GA)' abbreviation appearing in the governance section refers to the project's internal consortium governance body, not a Grant Agreement document.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents
section13_checks.check_notes: All call constraint and scope requirement citations embedded in the content (CC-03, CC-04, CC-05, CC-06, CC-07, CC-08, CC-10, SR-02, SR-03, SR-05c, SR-06, SR-07, SR-09, SR-10) are confirmed in validation_status CL-11 (status: confirmed) with source_ref pointing to Tier 2B docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json and docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json. The TRL range claim (TRL 2 to TRL 5) is confirmed in CL-12 with source_ref to Tier 2B call_constraints.json (CC-01). Both Tier 2B paths appear in traceability_footer.primary_sources[].

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, equipment — not present in Tier 3
section13_checks.check_notes: All partner names (ATU, CERIA, BIIS, ISIA, NIHS, ELI, BAL, FIIT), key personnel names (Prof. Dr. Lena Hoffmann, Dr. Stefan Braun, Dr. Claire Moreau, Prof. Dr. Pieter Dekker, Prof. Alessandro Ferretti, Prof. Karin Eriksson, Dr. Markus Steiner, Dr. Antti Korhonen, Dr. Isabel Navarro), organisational types, nationalities, capabilities and infrastructure (128 A100 GPUs, ERC Advanced Grant, Industry 4.0 pilot factory, de-identified clinical data repository of 3M records, EuroHPC access, multi-agent simulation testbed, etc.) are confirmed in validation_status CL-09 and CL-10 (both status: confirmed) with source_refs pointing to Tier 3 docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json, capabilities.json, and roles.json. All three Tier 3 consortium files appear in traceability_footer.primary_sources[]. WP lead assignments (CL-10) are cross-verified against Tier 4 wp_structure.json. The 'each partner's expertise is uniquely matched to their WP leadership role as evidenced in Tier 3 capabilities.json, partners.json, and roles.json' statement at the end of B.3.2 explicitly declares the Tier 3 grounding.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Commencing any Phase 8 activity — including preparatory drafting — before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed
section13_checks.check_notes: The artifact carries schema_id orch.tier5.implementation_section.v1 (begins with 'orch.tier5.'), making it a Phase 8 artifact. Validation_status CL-01 (status: confirmed) asserts 'Budget gate passed before drafting commenced' with source_ref explicitly identifying Tier 4 docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json with gate_pass_declaration: pass. The budget_gate_assessment.json path appears in traceability_footer.primary_sources[] (tier: 4). CL-14 (status: confirmed) records that no specific budget figures (person-months, costs) are cited in proposal text. Scanning sub-section content confirms no patterns of the form '€[0-9]+', '[0-9]+ EUR', or '[0-9]+ person-months' used as confirmed budget values. The STAFF EFFORT paragraph in B.3.1 states 'Specific person-month figures per participant per WP are recorded in Table 3.1f in the Part A budget tables, consistent with the validated budget' — a forward reference to Part A tables, not an assertion of specific figures in the proposal text.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4
section13_checks.check_notes: Not directly verifiable from artifact content alone. This prohibition governs the orchestration process, not the artifact produced. The artifact itself references multiple Tier 4 phase output paths as primary_sources, evidencing that decisions were externalised to Tier 4. Enforcement of this prohibition is the responsibility of the decision-log-update skill and the broader orchestration state management, not this artifact-level audit.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding its scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic
section13_checks.check_notes: Not directly verifiable from artifact content alone. This prohibition governs workflow and skill execution behaviour, not deliverable content. No language in the artifact's content attempts to redefine phase purposes, tier definitions, or gate logic. The artifact correctly defers budget gate authority to Tier 4 phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json rather than asserting gate logic itself.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete
section13_checks.check_notes: The artifact references only upstream (lower-numbered) phase outputs as inputs: Phase 3 (wp_structure.json), Phase 4 (gantt.json), Phase 6 (implementation_architecture.json), Phase 7 (budget_gate_assessment.json). All are confirmed prior phases. No forward references to higher-numbered phase outputs are used as inputs to this artifact. The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] and validation_status claim_statuses are consistent: no Phase 5 outputs are used as structural inputs here (Phase 5 impact architecture is appropriately consumed by the impact section, not the implementation section).

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable
section13_checks.check_notes: validation_status.overall_status is 'confirmed'. All 16 claim_statuses are either 'confirmed' (CL-01 through CL-15) or 'inferred' (CL-16). No claim carries status 'unresolved'. CL-16 (inferred) uses an explicitly stated inference chain: 'Tier 4 governs over Tier 3 per CLAUDE.md authority hierarchy (Section 3)' to resolve a minor discrepancy between wp_structure.json (Tier 4) and roles.json (Tier 3) for ISIA, NIHS, and ELI WP participation. The inference chain satisfies §12.2 (Inferred category: derived by logical reasoning from confirmed evidence; inference chain stated). No content in either sub-section contains language suggesting unresolved gaps, contradictions, or dependency on unvalidated data that would require '[DATA REQUIRED]' or 'INCOMPLETE' flagging.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible
section13_checks.violation_evidence: The artifact's traceability_footer.primary_sources[] contains no paths within docs/tier1_normative_framework/extracted/. Despite this, the artifact makes the following claims that reference Horizon Europe programme rules and regulatory compliance obligations without any Tier 1 normative framework source references: (1) Participation condition claim — B.3.2 ELI partner description states 'Switzerland is an associated country eligible for Horizon Europe participation per CC-08'; this is a Horizon Europe participation condition traced only to Tier 2B (call_constraints.json) with no direct Tier 1 path reference for the underlying eligibility rule (Framework Programme Regulation / Association Agreement provisions); (2) Regulatory compliance claim — B.3.1 WP4 T4-03 description states 'auditable interaction traces supporting explainability, accountability, and regulatory compliance including EU AI Act high-risk system obligations'; (3) Regulatory compliance claim — B.3.1 RISK-08 states 'EU AI Act compliance obligations for high-risk AI systems (particularly the healthcare demonstrator) may impose additional development effort'; (4) Regulatory compliance claim — B.3.2 NIHS partner description states 'NIHS holds responsibility for healthcare ethics oversight (GDPR compliance for special category health data, Categories 2, 4, and 8 management)'. None of these claims carry a primary_sources entry pointing to docs/tier1_normative_framework/extracted/. The participation condition for Switzerland (CC-08) is the clearest §13.9 case as it is a Horizon Europe participation condition asserted without a Tier 1 traceability path in the footer.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4
section13_checks.check_notes: traceability_footer.no_unsupported_claims_declaration is true. validation_status.overall_status is 'confirmed'. Examining all 16 claim_statuses: none carry status 'unresolved' with an absent source_ref. All claims are either confirmed (with named Tier 2–4 source references) or inferred (CL-16, with an explicit stated inference chain identifying the tier authority applied). The primary_sources array contains 12 entries spanning Tier 2 (4 entries: Tier 2B call_constraints.json, scope_requirements.json; Tier 2A section_schema_registry.json, evaluator_expectation_registry.json), Tier 3 (4 entries: partners.json, roles.json, capabilities.json, selected_call.json), and Tier 4 (4 entries: budget_gate_assessment.json, wp_structure.json, gantt.json, implementation_architecture.json). The §13.9 violation is a traceability-completeness finding (absent Tier 1 paths for regulatory claims) but does not constitute a §13.10 violation because the no_unsupported_claims_declaration is declared true and no claim_status is 'unresolved' with a missing source_ref.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions
section13_checks.check_notes: Not checkable from a phase output or deliverable artifact. This prohibition governs write operations to Tier 1 and Tier 2 source documents, which cannot be detected by reading a Tier 5 deliverable artifact. The artifact reads from Tier 2A and Tier 2B extracted files (as declared in traceability_footer.primary_sources[]) but does not write to them. Structurally non-applicable to artifact-level audit.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional
section13_checks.check_notes: Not directly checkable from artifact content alone. This prohibition governs agent and skill execution behaviour, not deliverable content. Affirmative evidence within the artifact: validation_status CL-14 (status: confirmed) explicitly cites 'Constraint compliance: CLAUDE.md Section 8.3 and 8.4' as a source reference for the budget figure abstention claim, demonstrating that the producing agent treated CLAUDE.md as a governing authority during drafting. No content in the artifact conflicts with CLAUDE.md constitutional requirements.
